text.title: Bug Pattern to Lint Rule Conversion Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure from "we keep seeing this bug" to "rule wired in pre-commit + decision-record committed". Decision-gates block on threshold and FP failures.

step.name: cluster-tickets
step.input: recent bug tickets (last 30/90 days), tag / error-class / file-path metadata.
step.action: Group tickets by symptom + file glob. Identify clusters of ≥3 instances in 30 days OR ≥5 in 90 days.
step.decision-gate: If cluster size < threshold — STOP, verdict block-low-recurrence.

step.name: draft-detector
step.action: Pick detector kind: ruff (Python), ESLint (JS/TS), regex (cross-language), AST visitor (deep checks), test-name (post-condition). Draft and unit-test against the cluster samples.
step.decision-gate: If no mechanical detector is feasible — STOP, verdict block-no-detector.

step.name: false-positive-scan
step.action: Run the detector across the historical codebase. Count fires; classify each as TP (real bug-shape) or FP (benign). Compute fp_rate_pct = FP / total_fires × 100.
step.decision-gate: If fp_rate_pct > 5 — refine pattern OR STOP with verdict block-fp-too-high.

step.name: wire-into-pre-commit
step.action: Add the detector to the pre-commit hook configuration. Update CI to fail on detector hits. Document the rule's purpose in CHANGELOG.md / decisions/.
step.decision-gate: If hook does not block a known-bad sample — STOP, wiring is broken.

step.name: emit-record
step.action: Commit decision-record JSON to `decisions/`. Schedule quarterly review (calendar / cron). Send notice to owner_email.
step.decision-gate: last_reviewed must equal today; owner_email must be reachable.
